name: list-tabular
description: Hairline-ruled ledger of items — name on the left, body on the right.
purpose: Use for compact reference tables: glossary-style entries, key/value pairs, specs. Four primary variants (def, metric, spec, register) tune the visual treatment; secondary modifiers (rule, solid, stacked, outline) refine each.

rows.description: Each list item is one row. Lead each with **Name.** then the description/value.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: list-tabular -->

## Slide heading.

- **First entry.** Description or value for the first entry.
- **Second entry.** Description or value for the second entry.
- **Third entry.** Description or value for the third entry.
- **Fourth entry.** Description or value for the fourth entry.
sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular -->

## The four substance contracts a component plugs into.

1. Prose
   - Headings, paragraphs, inline emphasis — Marp markdown into semantic HTML.
   - _CSS-only; no post-processor required_
2. Structure
   - Headings plus nested lists with conventions; a post-processor rewrites the list into purpose-built DOM.
   - _Per-component transform.js in lib/components_
3. Series
   - Tabular DSL — axes and datapoints as bullets, parsed into geometry.
   - _Chart-family kernel (radar, quadrant, piechart, gantt, kanban)_
4. Graph
   - External graph language (Mermaid today; D2 or PlantUML in the future).
   - _External CLI; palette injected at build time_

whenToUse.title: Compact reference rows
whenToUse.body: Five or more rows where each row is a name plus a short description or value. Glossary-style entries, key/value pairs, technical specs.

whenToUse.title: Pick one primary variant
whenToUse.body: `def` for editorial, `metric` for tiled values, `spec` for technical keys, `register` for tagged pills. Default (no variant) is the hairline ledger.

whenToUse.title: Numbered automatically
whenToUse.body: Author as `ol` (`1.` source). The leading column is the counter — `def` and `spec.stacked` enlarge it to span both rows.

antiPatterns.title: Three or fewer rows
antiPatterns.body: The ledger needs density to justify its shape. For two or three items, reach for cards-stack or cards-wide — the rows get the room to breathe.

antiPatterns.title: Long per-row prose
antiPatterns.body: Each row is a name plus a sentence. If the description runs two or three sentences, move to cards-wide or split across slides.

antiPatterns.title: Stacking two primary variants
antiPatterns.body: `def`, `metric`, `spec`, and `register` are mutually exclusive. Pair each only with its secondary modifier (def+rule, metric+solid, spec+stacked, register+outline).

related.name: glossary
related.when: term/definition pairs with auto-derived range pill

related.name: cards-stack
related.when: two or three richer items, not a ledger

related.name: actors
related.when: the left column is a named person, not a key

related.name: principles
related.when: rows are stated rules, not reference entries

related.name: list
related.when: rows are bullets without a label-plus-description shape

def.label: Editorial (def)
def.caption: Big counter spans both rows; eyebrow above the name. Use when the entries are conceptual definitions that want editorial weight.
def.sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular def -->

## The four layers of the design system.

1. Function `Purpose`
   - Why the slide exists — the communication job it does. Seven families.
2. Form `Composition`
   - The spatial shape the slide takes. Eleven shapes.
3. Substance `Data`
   - The kind of content that fills the shape. Four contracts.
4. Finish `Treatment`
   - The palette, typography, and chrome applied last. Theme-controlled.

metric.label: Tile (metric)
metric.caption: Meta renders as a bordered tile on the right — useful when each row carries a numeric value or a status the audience scans.
metric.sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular metric -->

## Renderer parity — current scoreboard.

1. Marp CLI build path `334 / 334`
2. lattice-emulator inline path `334 / 334`
3. marp-vscode runtime DOM path `327 / 334`
4. Cross-renderer page-count parity `pass`

spec.label: Technical key (spec)
spec.caption: Mono name as a key, accent-coloured. Technical-reference feel — config keys, API parameters, environment flags.
spec.sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular spec -->

## Environment flags the build path reads.

1. `LATTICE_THEME` `string`
   - Override the deck's declared theme at build time. Default: theme from front-matter.
2. `LATTICE_CACHE` `0 | 1`
   - Toggle the render helper's hash-keyed cache. Default: 1 locally, 0 on CI.
3. `LATTICE_TRACE` `0 | 1`
   - Emit per-slide transform timing to stderr. Default: 0.

register.label: Tagged pill (register)
register.caption: Meta renders as an accent-soft pill — status registers, tagged inventories, role registers where the meta is a category.
register.sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular register -->

## Active components — release status.

1. cards-grid `stable`
2. split-brief `stable`
3. radar-chart `beta`
4. quadrant-chart `beta`
5. kanban-board `alpha`

rule.label: def + rule
rule.caption: Adds a continuous accent rail down the left edge of `def`. Anchors the column visually when the entries are long and the eye needs a guide.
rule.sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular def rule -->

## The four layers of the design system.

1. Function `Purpose`
   - Why the slide exists — the communication job it does. Seven families.
2. Form `Composition`
   - The spatial shape the slide takes. Eleven shapes.
3. Substance `Data`
   - The kind of content that fills the shape. Four contracts.
4. Finish `Treatment`
   - The palette, typography, and chrome applied last. Theme-controlled.

solid.label: metric + solid
solid.caption: Fills the metric tile with accent colour instead of the bordered default. Use when the values are headline numbers the room should land on first.
solid.sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular metric solid -->

## Quarterly headline metrics.

1. Net new ARR `$4.2M`
2. Logo retention `94%`
3. Time-to-value (median) `11d`
4. Pipeline coverage `3.2x`

stacked.label: spec + stacked
stacked.caption: Description drops to its own row beneath the spec name. Counter enlarges to span both rows. Use when the description is longer than one line.
stacked.sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular spec stacked -->

## API endpoints exposed by the deck-server.

1. `GET /decks/:id` `200 | 404`
   - Returns the rendered deck metadata, slide manifest, and signed PDF URL.
2. `POST /decks/:id/render` `202 | 409`
   - Enqueues a re-render. 409 if a render is already in flight for this deck.
3. `DELETE /decks/:id/cache` `204 | 404`
   - Evicts the cached PDF and forces a cold re-render on the next read.

outline.label: register + outline
outline.caption: Renders the register tag as a hairline-bordered outline pill instead of the filled accent-soft default. Lighter visual weight when the deck has many register slides in a row.
outline.sample:
<!-- _class: list-tabular register outline -->

## Active components — release status.

1. cards-grid `stable`
2. split-brief `stable`
3. radar-chart `beta`
4. quadrant-chart `beta`
5. kanban-board `alpha`
